reviews.review_msg: Grading scheme was weird.

His exams are BRUTALLY RANDOM. He expects students to have a mastery of the entire concept and that would barely get you a passing grade. literally zero way to study for his exams. he givess practice midterms. but his real exam is completely different. I get he doesn't give us exact replicas. but give us SOMETHING CLOSE! its not fair at all! AVOID!

He doesn't even know how his students should study for his own exams because they r so over the place. Don't bother with any practice midterms or lectures. best u could do is try to understand the homework but even then you'll probably fail his exams. Ur completely in the dark for what the exams might test, if you want a will live avoid this prof.

Provides engaging lecture, clearly cares about students. That being said, I'm not a big fan of his exam style as the wording is often very confusing and they're EXTREMELY time constrained. The assignments are also very redundant and lengthy, and not particularly useful for preparing for the exams. Great professor but hard to get good marks.

Okay so during lectures he goes over important statistic theory... which is good... but it all ends there. Exams are nothing at all like what you would expect. over 70% of the classes failed the midterm... He puts very niche and just pointless questions on the exam which dont make sense. He's passionate abt stats but the tests are just so strange.

The exams are nonsense; he won't curve, but more than 70% of the class can't even pass his midterms!! Absolute joke of a course, nothing he teaches is ever on the exams, but rather full of niche, random, super-specific questions that test no meaningful learning. If you want a decent grade, do not take this course with him
Poorly designed exams and boring assignments. Barely any material to prepare for the niche and specific questions. Assignments are assigned late regularly.

Extremely tricky tests, hard to get high marks. Midterms are 50 minutes each, but most of people cannot finish all. Tests are out of 22, counts 17% of your final grade, almost 1:1. There are two MC questions, 2 marks each, but answers are a-e, have to select all correct answers to get full marks, avoid him if you dont need to take this course.

My experience - Great prof overall, would love to have again in another course - Very enjoyable, informative, and engaging lectures which had a fair amount of humour (also recorded) - Many quizzes, but manageable, and topics and dates were informed of well-beforehand - Didn't end up using the textbook that often, so get online if possible.

Disliked the grading scheme, only final and quizzes counted for grades (no HW marks).Each of the quizzes were worth 15%, and if you did bad on 1 of them then you would lose a solid chunk of your grade right there, even if do well on the final and show that you end up understanding the topics there are no adjustments to grading to show your learning

Super funny and entertaining lectures that I learned a lot from, and his slides are well written. Very approachable too, and good at explaining concepts or questions you're stuck on. Only gripe was the harsh grading scheme, but the assessments were fair and corresponded to the homework and slides. Take a class with him if you can!!

great lecturer. 4 quizzes worth 15% each and a final, but quizzes are 20 mins each and diabolical. class averages on canvas for quizzes are q1= 53%, q2=58%, q3=62%, q4=63% and that was after he removed 1-2 marks so class average was even lower before. update** quizzes were hard but final was extremely fair, take the prof LOL

I really enjoyed STAT 285 with Tom. His lectures were interesting, and his unique perspectives on the content helped me understand in a way 270 did not. That being said, the quizzes can be tight for time, and although this was not a huge issue for me, it can definitely be one if you struggle with quick decision making. However, I loved this class!

His teaching is a bit confusing but he seems to be an enthusiastic prof. His class has 4 quizzes (worth 15% each), I would not say the content he tested is super challenging, I find them to be fair. The brutal part is the time limit (20 mins), if the quizzes were 30-40 mins long and had more questions, I think the average would be higher.

I respect his teaching philosophies, especially his emphasis on understanding over computation. Lectures are solid, uses real life examples which help solidify concepts. Shows R code which was interesting. However, quizzes (15% each) are very mean. Difficult, not enough time, and doesn't scale much. Average currently is 60% after scale. Beware!
many lectures and also homework
His grading scheme is too strict and weird.
"There are 2 kinds of people in the world. Those that can infer from missing information..."

This course is 90% exam based with a bunch of HW! Exams are open book but are quite tricky with a pop question at the end that you can only solve if you attend lectures. Personally I hated it, but it's over.
Honestly, didn't care bout this class much but exams were a joke. Forget about the fact that they're open book since that ain't gonna help ya.
